data.title: Tautogram checker
data.solution:
import sys
import math

# Auto-generated code below aims at helping you parse
# the standard input according to the problem statement.

text = input().lower()
first_letter = text[0]

same = True
for word in text.split():
    if word[0] != first_letter:
        same = False

# Write an answer using print
# To debug: print("Debug messages...", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)

print(same)
data.statement: A tautogram is a text in which every word starts with the same letter, ignoring case. Your goal is to check whether the given input text is a tautogram.

testCases.title: Test 2
testCases.testIn: Betty bought a bit of butter

testCases.title: Validator 2
testCases.testIn: But the butter Betty bought was bitter

testCases.title: Test 4
testCases.testIn: Truly they triumph, trumpeting trills to trounce the terrible travesties.

data.constraints:
[[text]] always contains at least two words, separated by a single space.
Each word starts with a letter.
data.inputDescription: <<Line 1 :>> A string [[text]] containing the text to check.
data.outputDescription: {{True}} whether the given text is a tautogram, {{False}} otherwise.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

A tautogram is a text in which every word starts with the same letter, ignoring case. Your goal is to check whether the given input text is a tautogram.
Input
Line 1 : A string text containing the text to check.
Output
True whether the given text is a tautogram, False otherwise.
Constraints
text always contains at least two words, separated by a single space.
Each word starts with a letter.
Example
Input
She sells seashells
Output
True
